samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit divider using a combination of sequential and combinational logic.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal for initializing the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit flag indicating whether the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input for the dividend.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input for the divisor.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation signal to initiate the division.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">Signal indicating that the 16-bit result is ready to be consumed.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">Output signal indicating the result is valid and ready to be consumed.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output for the division result, containing both the quotient and remainder.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUB">1</parameter>
        <parameter id="ADD">2</parameter>
        <parameter id="SHIFT">3</parameter>
        <parameter id="SIGN">4</parameter>
        <parameter id="DONE">5</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rem_1">Remainder.</rem>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="quot_1">Quotient.</quot>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="neg_divisor_1">Negative divisor.</neg_divisor_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sr_1">Shift register.</sr_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count_1">Counter.</count_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sign_1">Sign flag.</sign_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="done_1">Done flag.</done_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="opn_valid_1">Valid operation signal.</opn_valid_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="res_ready_1">Result ready signal.</res_ready_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sign_2">Sign flag.</sign_2>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="dividend_2">Dividend.</dividend_2>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="divisor_2">Divisor.</divisor_2>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="res_valid_1">Result valid signal.</res_valid_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="result_1">Result.</result_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="temp_1">Temporary variable.</temp_1>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="temp_2">Temporary variable.</temp_2>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="temp_3">Temporary variable.</temp_3>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="temp_4">Temporary variable.</temp_4>

samples.tb_log:
[COMPILE verilator+cpp]
%Warning-WIDTH: div_8bit.v:33:16: Operator ASSIGNDLY expects 16 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's REPLICATE generates 9 bits.
                                : ... In instance div_8bit
   33 |             SR <= {dividend_abs, 1'b0};
      |                ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-UNUSED: div_8bit.v:17:15: Signal is not used: 'NEG_DIVISOR'
                                 : ... In instance div_8bit
   17 |     reg [7:0] NEG_DIVISOR;
      |               ^~~~~~~~~~~
%Warning-COMBDLY: div_8bit.v:61:20: Delayed assignments (<=) in non-clocked (non flop or latch) block
                                  : ... Suggest blocking assignments (=)
   61 |             result <= SR[15:0];
      |                    ^~
                  *** See the manual before disabling this,
                  else you may end up with different sim results.
%Error-BLKANDNBLK: div_8bit.v:11:23: Unsupported: Blocked and non-blocking assignments to same variable: 'result'
   11 |     output reg [15:0] result
      |                       ^~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
